onboarding.question: While specifying the token expiry mid-session flow in AC#27, I found that "the Not signed in indicator reappears automatically" uses no numeric bound on "automatically." This requires the product spec to be revised to replace "automatically" with a specific duration (e.g. "within 200ms of access token expiry detection") so engineers have a testable target for the re-appearance latency.
onboarding.recommendations:
1. My recommendation: The "Not signed in" indicator reappears within 500ms of access token expiry detection on the client.
→ Rationale: 500ms is fast enough to feel immediate to the user while giving the client a realistic window to detect the token expiry event, update auth state, and re-render the indicator — well within the threshold where users perceive a UI change as responsive rather than delayed.

Updating AC#27 now.

---

**AC#27 — revised text:**

*"If a user's authentication token expires mid-session, the "Not signed in" indicator reappears within 500ms of access token expiry detection on the client and all chat functionality becomes read-only — the user can view conversation history but cannot send new messages; attempting to send a message triggers an inline error; the user can sign in again via the Auth Sheet without losing conversation history."*
